comment: Premise-level source/provenance audit against the cached EC 2004 source text. Each audit-premise from Assumptions.lean is judged individually; the remaining Section 3 helper-certificate premises are visible partial boundaries where Lean still assumes selected min/max rounded-type fields.

assumption_nonnegative_alpha_bound.reason: Theorem 2.1 uses alpha as the maximum marginal utility; monotonicity of the source valuations makes this bound nonnegative.

halpha_nonneg : 0 ≤ alpha.source_location: Derived from monotone source utilities and alpha as the maximum marginal utility
halpha_nonneg : 0 ≤ alpha.reason: Every marginal utility is nonnegative under the source monotonicity assumption, so the maximum marginal utility alpha is nonnegative.

assumption_alpha_marginal_value_bound.reason: The alpha-bounded form of Theorem 2.1 assumes every marginal value is at most alpha; the source's named maximum-marginal version is the closed specialization.

hbound : MarginalBound v alpha.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:160-166: alpha is the maximum marginal utility / an upper bound on every marginal item value
hbound : MarginalBound v alpha.reason: The Lean premise is the explicit upper-bound form of the source maximum-marginal condition.

assumption_duplicate_free_goods_enumerat.reason: The constructive algorithm allocates the finite set of goods one-by-one; Lean represents that finite set by a duplicate-free list.

hnodup : goodsList.Nodup.source_location: Finite-set/list representation of the source goods set M
hnodup : goodsList.Nodup.reason: Duplicate-freeness is not an extra mathematical assumption; it ensures the Lean list enumerates the paper's finite set of goods exactly.

assumption_positive_atom_bound.reason: The measure-valued extension separates the alpha = 0 divisible case and proves the finite partition route for alpha > 0.

halpha_pos : 0 < alpha.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:263-267: The case alpha = 0 is handled separately; for alpha > 0 reduce to indivisible pieces
halpha_pos : 0 < alpha.reason: The Lean endpoint is the positive-alpha branch of the source Theorem 2.3 proof.

assumption_ptas_error_parameter_range.reason: The PTAS/FPTAS theorem is stated for positive error epsilon; the exact finite arithmetic endpoint restricts to epsilon <= 1 without loss for the displayed small-error regime.
assumption_ptas_error_parameter_range.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:310-318 and Theorem 3.3 proof around lambda = 56/epsilon

hepsilon_pos : 0 < epsilon.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:310-318: PTAS/FPTAS definitions quantify over an error parameter epsilon > 0
hepsilon_pos : 0 < epsilon.reason: This is the standard source theorem domain for approximation schemes.

hepsilon_le_one : epsilon ≤ 1.source_location: Finite arithmetic wrapper for the source small-epsilon PTAS regime
hepsilon_le_one : epsilon ≤ 1.reason: For epsilon >= 1 the approximation guarantee is weaker/easier; the Lean exact constant proof records the nontrivial epsilon <= 1 case.

assumption_external_graham_scheduling_bo.reason: The source invokes Graham/Coffman-Langston scheduling approximation by citation; Lean proves the fair-division consequence from that external scheduling certificate, so the cited scheduling theorem remains a known partial/external-library boundary rather than a source caveat.

C : Theorem32.Graham14SchedulingApproxim.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:332-337 and 367-370: Graham/Coffman-Langston scheduling approximation is invoked by citation
C : Theorem32.Graham14SchedulingApproxim.reason: The Lean endpoint proves the fair-division consequence from the scheduling approximation certificate; the reusable scheduling theorem remains the existing Section 3 partial boundary, not a source caveat.

assumption_fixed_dimension_ip_runtime_bo.reason: The final PTAS/FPTAS runtime conclusion uses Lenstra fixed-dimension integer programming, which remains an explicit reusable-library partial boundary rather than a source caveat.

cert : Theorem33.RoundedInstanceSearchCe.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:393-405 and 501-536: finite rounded-type IP search and fixed-dimension IP runtime layer
cert : Theorem33.RoundedInstanceSearchCe.reason: The Lean endpoint is conditional on the rounded-instance search certificate; this is the already-documented Section 3 IP/solver partial boundary, not a source caveat.

assumption_positive_rounding_and_load_pa.reason: The rounding construction uses positive average load L, rounded average LR, and a positive lambda chosen as O(1/epsilon).

hlambda : 1 < lambda.source_location: Derived from the source choice lambda = 56/epsilon in the nontrivial 0 < epsilon <= 1 regime
hlambda : 1 < lambda.reason: The exact Lean endpoint uses lambda > 1 for rounding arithmetic; it follows from the paper's lambda choice.

hlambda : 0 < lambda.source_location: Derived from lambda = O(1/epsilon) with epsilon > 0
hlambda : 0 < lambda.reason: Positive lambda is a direct consequence of the source rounding parameter.

hL : 0 < L.source_location: Derived from the source positive job/good values and average load definition
hL : 0 < L.reason: The identical-utilities scheduling model uses positive processing times/values; L is their positive average in the nontrivial instance.

hLR : 0 < LR.source_location: Derived from L <= LR and 0 < L in the rounded instance construction
hLR : 0 < LR.reason: The rounded average remains positive because it dominates the positive original average.

assumption_base_load_at_most_rounded_ave.reason: The source explicitly states that the original average load is at most the rounded average load.

hbase_le_avg : L ≤ LR.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:428-434: It is easy to see that L <= LR
hbase_le_avg : L ≤ LR.reason: The Lean premise is the displayed comparison between original and rounded average loads.

assumption_claim34_positive_small_goods_.reason: Claim 3.4 is stated after reducing to goods with positive value below L.

hgoods_pos : ∀ g : SourceItem, g ∈ goods.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:337-346: jobs/goods have positive processing time/value
hgoods_pos : ∀ g : SourceItem, g ∈ goods.reason: The identical-utilities scheduling reduction uses positive item values.

hgoods_lt : ∀ g : SourceItem, g ∈ goods.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:373-383: assume v(i) < L for every i
hgoods_lt : ∀ g : SourceItem, g ∈ goods.reason: This is the small-good domain immediately preceding Claim 3.4.

hitem_nonneg : ∀ A : Alloc, ∀ i : Source.source_location: Derived from positive source item values in the identical-utilities model
hitem_nonneg : ∀ A : Alloc, ∀ i : Source.reason: Nonnegativity is weaker than the source positive item-value condition.

hitem_lt : ∀ A : Alloc, ∀ i : SourceAgen.source_location: Derived from the source small-good condition v(i) < L for every good
hitem_lt : ∀ A : Alloc, ∀ i : SourceAgen.reason: Every item appearing in an allocated bundle is one of the source goods satisfying the small-good bound.

assumption_claim34_rounded_type_window_c.reason: The Claim 3.4 rounded finite-IP endpoint compares assignments whose rounded bundle types lie in the selected min/max load window.

∀ i : SourceAgent, typeOf i ∈ Theorem33..source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:437-446: Claim 3.4 restricts every bundle load to the selected L/2-to-2L window; lines 501-536 encode the rounded type search over that window
∀ i : SourceAgent, typeOf i ∈ Theorem33..reason: The Lean predicate is the finite rounded-type representation of the source bounded-load window for the selected min/max pair.

assumption_additive_transfer_load_window.reason: Lemma 3.5 and Claim 3.4 work in the half-load window L/2 < load < 2L; Lean exposes the positivity/nonnegativity consequences used by the algebraic transfer endpoint.

houtMin : 0 < outMin.source_location: Derived from the Lemma 3.5 half-load lower bound for the output allocation
houtMin : 0 < outMin.reason: A load above L/2 is positive because the rounded construction has positive L.

hroundedMin : 0 < roundedMin.source_location: Derived from the Lemma 3.5 rounded half-load lower bound
hroundedMin : 0 < roundedMin.reason: A rounded load above L/2 is positive because L is positive.

hoptMin : 0 < optMin.source_location: Derived from Claim 3.4 / Lemma 3.5 half-load window for the optimal allocation
hoptMin : 0 < optMin.reason: The bounded optimum has minimum load above L/2.

hoptMax : 0 ≤ optMax.source_location: Derived from nonnegative item values and the load definition
hoptMax : 0 ≤ optMax.reason: Maximum load is nonnegative in the nonnegative additive utility model.

hroundedMax_nonneg : 0 ≤ roundedMax.source_location: Derived from nonnegative rounded item values and the load definition
hroundedMax_nonneg : 0 ≤ roundedMax.reason: Rounded maximum load is nonnegative in the rounded additive model.

hroundedMin_half : L ≤ 2 * roundedMin.source_location: Algebraic form of L/2 < roundedMin from Lemma 3.5
hroundedMin_half : L ≤ 2 * roundedMin.reason: Lean uses the non-strict doubled form of the source lower half-load inequality.

hroundedMax_half : L ≤ 2 * roundedMax.source_location: Derived from the rounded allocation half-load window
hroundedMax_half : L ≤ 2 * roundedMax.reason: The source window applies to every player load, including the maximum rounded load.

hoptMin_half : L ≤ 2 * optMin.source_location: Algebraic form of L/2 < optMin from Claim 3.4
hoptMin_half : L ≤ 2 * optMin.reason: Lean uses the non-strict doubled form of the source lower half-load inequality.

hoptMax_half : L ≤ 2 * optMax.source_location: Derived from the bounded optimum half-load window
hoptMax_half : L ≤ 2 * optMax.reason: The source window applies to every player load, including the maximum optimal load.

assumption_uniform_random_weight_normali.reason: Theorem 4.2 assumes normalized nonnegative additive utilities with every item value at most alpha and proves a bound for any positive threshold parameter.

halpha : 0 ≤ alpha.source_location: Derived from alpha as an upper bound on nonnegative item values
halpha : 0 ≤ alpha.reason: The source item weights are nonnegative, so their upper bound alpha is nonnegative in the nontrivial theorem domain.

ht : 0 < t.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:679-681: Chebyshev bound is stated for t > 0
ht : 0 < t.reason: The Lean probability bound keeps the source positive threshold parameter explicit.

hnonneg : ∀ p : Agent, ∀ g : Item, 0 ≤ w.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:655-660: utilities are additive and normalized to total one
hnonneg : ∀ p : Agent, ∀ g : Item, 0 ≤ w.reason: The finite weight model represents nonnegative additive item utilities.

hsum : ∀ p : Agent, ∑ g : Item, w p g =.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:655-660: assume the sum of utilities of each player over all goods is one
hsum : ∀ p : Agent, ∑ g : Item, w p g =.reason: This is exactly the normalization used in Theorem 4.2.

hbound : ∀ p : Agent, ∀ g : Item, w p g.source_location: LMMS04FairDivision.txt:659-661: Suppose v_{p,i} <= alpha for all players and goods
hbound : ∀ p : Agent, ∀ g : Item, w p g.reason: This is the source item-value upper-bound condition.
